metric_name: Software development as primary IS challenge
metric_value: Development expertise constraining overall industry growth despite new tools
notes: 4GLs, data management systems, GUI builders introduced; majority still constrained by COBOL/proprietary expertise

metric_value: Mega enterprise repository is failed concept
notes: Individual toolset repositories work; waiting to deploy until all entity relationships defined in large project is recipe for disaster

metric_value: 3-5 years out for production systems from 1993
notes: Still early for mainstream MIS; watch OMG developments; 1993 too early to standardize on any OO supplier

metric_name: OO production systems mainstream actual timing

Will revolutionize industry by end of 1990s
notes: Not market but method/design strategy; structured modular design; reusable object libraries

metric_value: [UNVERIFIED]
Revenue leader; Oracle7 aggressive positioning
notes: Oracle Forms as business logic layer example using 4GL with C exit for optimization

metric_value: Well-positioned as client-server advocate; SQLServer remarketed by Microsoft
notes: Middleware-layer products also well-known

metric_value: Less visible in market since ASK Computer acquisition
notes: Traditionally offered advanced functionality and stable robust RDBMS

metric_value: Traditional strength in DEC and HP installed base
notes: Leader among four 4GL companies for SQL-based decision support and distributed applications

metric_value: Dramatic revenue growth last several quarters; excellent ISV platform reputation
notes: Growing strongly as ISV community platform technology

metric_value: Widely used IBM mainframe; recent thrust to HP 9000 and NCR System 3000
notes: Expanding from IBM mainframe strength to midrange servers

metric_name: C language role in 1993 development strategy
metric_value: Viable 3GL component; mostly-tools-some-C multi-component strategy emerging
notes: Aberdeen does not expect majority of commercial MIS programmers to write all new apps in C

metric_value: Language of choice for large-scale mainframe; data centers in maintenance mode
notes: MicroFocus COBOL under pressure from C language

metric_value: Disappointing; being reworked
notes: IBM in midst of reworking disappointing AD/Cycle strategies

metric_value: Leading Windows GUI builder for PC development
notes: Point-and-click; multiple SQL RDBMS backend support; production system GUI tool

metric_value: Full-blown integrated client-server for complex transaction processing
notes: Different class from Powersoft/Gupta; targeted at complex enterprise OLTP

metric_name: GUI support cost challenge for ISVs
metric_value: Major GUI alternatives: Windows, Macintosh, Motif, OpenLook; ISVs struggling with multi-GUI expense
notes: Automated toolkits being developed to create GUI at runtime to address multi-platform cost

metric_value: Core enterprise data still in hierarchical IMS files; SQL bridges required
notes: Despite SQL progress, accessing legacy hierarchical structures remains difficult

metric_value: MIS buyers confused by huge variety of client-server toolsets
notes: Bottom line: acquire toolsets capable of scaling to enterprise applications with mission-critical robustness

metric_value: No universally satisfactory client; organizations dealing with Windows, OS/2, Mac, Unix
notes: Huge benefit to tools that minimize effort to port and support multiple platforms

metric_value: Enhance legacy systems + create strategic applications + continuous staff training
notes: Black-box reengineering translator vision failed; most redeployment is porting and rehosting

metric_value: Not always necessary to select RDBMS supplier's toolkit; can supplement or replace
notes: RDBMS decision should be separated from toolset decision; independent 4GLs can leverage best RDBMS performance

metric_value: Standardizing types of service requests objects universally provide; object behavior in distributed systems
notes: OMG headquartered outside Boston; consortium of suppliers and large MIS organizations

metric_value: European BKS Poet, Object Design, Objectivity, Ontos, Servio, Versant offer OODBMS
notes: OODBMS supports variety of file/record formats: audio, engineering drawings, text

metric_value: CDD+ repository large base; strategy uncertain post-reorganization
notes: Role and character of DEC software development strategy still unclear

metric_value: C continued emphasis; gradual migration to object orientation in ISV community
notes: C and C++ clearly languages of choice for new application development especially ISV community
